@@ -173,9 +173,8 @@ active).
 
 ## Step 6: Share the fixture across multiple tests
 
-Suites let you reuse the same fixture instance for a group of tests. Define a
-suite in a directory-level `test.yaml`; every test beneath that directory joins
-the suite automatically.
+Suites keep a fixture alive for several tests. Declare one in a directory-level
+`test.yaml`; every descendant test joins automatically.
 
 ```yaml
 # tests/http/test.yaml
@@ -184,23 +183,26 @@ fixtures: [http]
 timeout: 45
 ```
 
-The harness activates the `http` fixture once, runs each test in
-`tests/http/`—including nested directories—in lexicographic order, and tears the
-fixture down afterwards. Suites are directory-owned, so move tests into a
-sibling directory if they should stay independent or give the subdirectory a
-different suite. Per-test frontmatter cannot declare `suite`; the harness raises
-an error to avoid mixing suite and non-suite executions in the same tree. Suite
-members also may not set their own `retry`; configure retries at the directory
-level in `test.yaml` when the entire suite should share the same policy.
+With that configuration the harness:
 
-Use selectors when you only want to run the suite while iterating:
+- Starts the `http` fixture once, runs all members in lexicographic order, and
+  tears the fixture down afterwards.
+- Pins the suite to a single worker while still running different suites (and
+  standalone tests) in parallel when `--jobs` permits it.
+- Keeps policies such as `fixtures`, `timeout`, and `retry` in the directory
+  defaults. Tests inside the suite cannot set `suite`, `fixtures`, or `retry` in
+  their own frontmatter; everything else (for example `inputs:` or additional
+  metadata) remains overridable per file. Outside of suites you can still use
+  frontmatter for any of those keys.
+
+Run the directory that owns the suite when you want to focus on it:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http
+uvx tenzir-test tests/http
 ```
 
-Running a single file from the suite still activates the shared fixture, making
-ad-hoc debugging straightforward without hand-written setup code.
+Selecting a single file inside that suite now fails fast with a descriptive error.
+This keeps the lifecycle predictable and avoids partially exercised fixtures.

 ### Stream raw output while iterating
 
-During early iterations you may want to inspect command output before you
-record reference artifacts. Enable passthrough mode to pipe the `tenzir`
-process output directly to your terminal while the harness still provisions
-fixtures and environment variables:
+During early iterations you may want to inspect command output before you record
+reference artifacts. Enable _passthrough mode_ via `--passthrough` (`-p`) to
+pipe the `tenzir` process output directly to your terminal while the harness
+still provisions fixtures and environment variables:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-test -p tests/alerts/high-severity.tql
+uvx tenzir-test --passthrough tests/high-severity.tql
 ```
 
 The harness enforces the exit code but skips comparisons, letting you decide

 ## Step 7: Introduce a fixture
 
@@ -180,43 +180,47 @@ the baseline via `--update` just like before.
 
 The fixture launches `tenzir-node` from the directory that owns the test file, so
 `tenzir-node.yaml` placed next to the scenario can refer to files with relative
-paths (for example `schemas/alerts.yaml` or `../inputs/alerts.ndjson`).
+paths (for example `../inputs/alerts.ndjson`).
 
-### Group fixture-backed tests into a suite
+### Reuse fixtures with suites
 
-When several tests should reuse the same fixture instance, promote the directory
-to a **suite** by setting `suite:` in its `test.yaml`. The suite runs each test
-sequentially under the shared fixtures before tearing them down.
+When several tests should share the same fixture lifecycle, promote their
+directory to a **suite**. Add `suite:` to the directory’s `test.yaml` and keep
+the fixture selection alongside the other defaults:
 
 ```yaml
 # tests/http/test.yaml
 suite: smoke-http
 fixtures: [http]
 timeout: 45
+retry: 2
 ```
 
-Everything under `tests/http/` (including nested directories) now belongs to the
-`smoke-http` suite. Suites are directory-owned: once you opt in, every
-descendant test participates. Move tests into a sibling directory if they should
-stay independent, or set a different suite at a deeper level. Per-test
-frontmatter cannot declare `suite`; the harness raises an error to avoid mixing
-suite and non-suite tests in the same tree. Likewise, tests inside the suite may
-not set `retry`—define it once in `test.yaml` so all members follow the same
-policy.
+Key behaviour:
+
+- Suites are directory-scoped. Once a `test.yaml` declares `suite`, every test
+  in that directory _and its subdirectories_ joins automatically. Move the
+  scenarios that should remain independent into a sibling directory.
+- Suites run sequentially on a single worker. The harness activates the shared
+  fixtures once, executes members in lexicographic order of their relative
+  paths, and tears the fixtures down afterwards. Other suites (and standalone
+  tests) still run in parallel when `--jobs` allows it.
+- Per-test frontmatter cannot introduce `suite`, and suite members may not
+  define their own `fixtures` or `retry`. Keep those policies in the directory
+  defaults so every member agrees on the shared lifecycle. Outside a suite,
+  frontmatter can still set `fixtures`, `retry`, or `timeout` as before.
+- Tests can override other keys (for example `inputs:` or additional metadata)
+  on a per-file basis when necessary.
 
-Suite members run in lexicographic order of their relative paths. The harness
-executes all suites before standalone tests and pins each suite to a single
-worker so shared fixtures activate only once. Use selectors to focus on a
-specific suite while iterating:
+Run the `http` directory that defines the suite when you iterate on it:
 
 ```sh
-uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http
+uvx tenzir-test tests/http
 ```
 
-Invoking a single file from the suite (`uvx tenzir-test
-tests/http/echo-read.tql`) still activates the suite fixtures, runs that test,
-and stops the fixture immediately afterwards—handy for debugging without
-manually juggling setup and teardown.
+Selecting a single file inside that suite fails fast with a descriptive error,
+which keeps the fixture lifecycle predictable and prevents partial runs from
+leaving shared state behind.
 
 ### Drive fixtures manually

@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@ my-package/
 
 ## CLI reference
 
-Run the suite from the project root:
+Run the tests from the project root:
 
 ```sh
 uvx tenzir-test
@@ -165,24 +165,20 @@ Key rules:
 - Suites are directory-owned. Once a `test.yaml` sets `suite`, all descendants
   belong to that suite. Put tests that should remain independent outside the
   suite directory or in a sibling directory with a different suite.
-- Per-test frontmatter may not declare `suite`. Mixing suite and non-suite tests
-  in the same directory tree raises a configuration error.
-- Tests inside a suite inherit the suite’s defaults (`fixtures`, `timeout`, and
-  other keys) and can still override or extend them in their own frontmatter.
-- Per-test frontmatter cannot set `retry` inside a suite; configure retries in
-  the suite’s `test.yaml` if you need them so every member follows the same
-  policy.
+- Per-test frontmatter may not declare `suite`.
+- Suite members inherit the directory defaults and can still override most keys
+  on a per-file basis. The exceptions are `fixtures` and `retry`, which must be
+  defined at the directory level once a suite is active so every member agrees
+  on the shared lifecycle. Outside suites you can still set those keys directly
+  in frontmatter.
 - The harness runs suite members sequentially in lexicographic order of their
   relative paths. Each suite occupies a single worker, but different suites can
   run in parallel when `--jobs` allows it.
 - The CLI executes all suites before any remaining standalone tests so shared
   fixtures start and stop predictably.
-- Target a specific suite with `uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http`. The
-  selection still accepts paths, so you can combine suite filters and explicit
-  directories.
-- Running an individual test file that belongs to a suite (`tenzir-test
-tests/http/echo-read.tql`) spins up the suite fixtures, runs that single test,
-  and tears the suite down immediately.
+- Run the directory that defines the suite (for example `tenzir-test tests/http`)
+  when you want to focus on it. Selecting an individual member now raises an
+  error so every run exercises the full lifecycle and shared fixtures stay in sync.
 
 ## Run a subset of tests
